inclusions.description: Worked ivory, bone, tortoise-shell, horn, antlers, coral, mother-of-pearl and other animal carving material, and articles of these materials (including articles obtained by moulding)

Worked vegetable or mineral carving material and articles of these materials; moulded or carved articles of wax, of stearin, of natural gums or natural resins or of modelling pastes, and other moulded or carved articles not elsewhere specified or included; worked, unhardened gelatin (except gelatin of heading 35.03) and articles of unhardened gelatin. Note 2: 'vegetable or mineral carving material' = (a) hard seeds, pips, hulls, nuts (e.g., corozo/dom); (b) amber, meerschaum, agglomerated amber/meerschaum, jet and mineral substitutes for jet

Brooms, brushes (including brushes constituting parts of machines, appliances or vehicles), hand-operated mechanical floor sweepers not motorised, mops and feather dusters; prepared knots and tufts for broom or brush making; paint pads and rollers; squeegees. Note 3: 'prepared knots and tufts for broom/brush making' = unmounted, ready for incorporation without division, or requiring only minor trimming
Hand sieves and hand riddles
Travel sets for personal toilet, sewing or shoe or clothes cleaning
Buttons, press-fasteners, snap-fasteners and press-studs, button moulds and other parts of these articles; button blanks
Slide fasteners and parts thereof

Ball point pens; felt tipped and other porous-tipped pens and markers; fountain pens, stylograph pens and other pens; duplicating stylos; propelling or sliding pencils; pen-holders, pencil-holders and similar holders; parts (including caps and clips) of the foregoing articles, other than those of heading 96.09
Pencils (other than pencils of heading 96.08), crayons, pencil leads, pastels, drawing charcoals, writing or drawing chalks and tailors' chalks
Slates and boards, with writing or drawing surfaces, whether or not framed

Date, sealing or numbering stamps, and the like (including devices for printing or embossing labels), designed for operating in the hand; hand-operated composing sticks and hand printing sets incorporating such composing sticks
Typewriter or similar ribbons, inked or otherwise prepared for giving impressions, whether or not on spools or in cartridges; ink-pads, whether or not inked, with or without boxes
Cigarette lighters and other lighters, whether or not mechanical or electrical, and parts thereof, other than flints and wicks
Smoking pipes (including pipe bowls) and cigar or cigarette holders, and parts thereof
Combs, hair-slides and the like; hairpins, curling pins, curling grips, hair-curlers and the like, other than those of heading 85.16, and parts thereof
Scent sprays and similar toilet sprays, and mounts and heads therefor; powder-puffs and pads for the application of cosmetics or toilet preparations
Vacuum flasks and other vacuum vessels, complete with cases; parts thereof other than glass inners
Tailors' dummies and other lay figures; automata and other animated displays used for shop window dressing
Sanitary towels (pads) and tampons, napkins and napkin liners for babies and similar articles, of any material
Monopods, bipods, tripods and similar articles

exclusions.condition: Pencils for cosmetic or toilet uses (e.g., eyebrow pencils, lip liner pencils)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 33 (cosmetics/toilet preparations) — Note 1(a). Cosmetic pencils → Ch.33; writing/drawing pencils → 96.09

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 66 (umbrellas, walking-sticks, parts thereof)

exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 71.17 — Note 1(c). Decorative brooches, novelty pins, fashion accessories that are imitation jewellery → 71.17

exclusions.condition: Parts of general use (Note 2 to Section XV) of base metal, or similar goods of plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XV or Chapter 39 — Note 1(d)

exclusions.condition: Cutlery or other articles of Chapter 82 WITH handles or parts of carving/moulding material — presented as complete articles
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 82 (cutlery/tools with carved handles) — Note 1(e). EXCEPTION: separately presented carved handles or parts of Ch.82 articles → 96.01 or 96.02

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 90 (spectacle frames, mathematical drawing pens, medical/dental/surgical/veterinary brushes)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 90 — Note 1(f): spectacle frames → 90.03; mathematical drawing pens → 90.17; specialised dental/medical brushes → 90.18

exclusions.condition: Articles of Chapter 91 (clock or watch cases)
Musical instruments or parts or accessories thereof
Articles of Chapter 93 (arms and parts thereof)
Articles of Chapter 94 (furniture, luminaires and lighting fittings)
Articles of Chapter 95 (toys, games, sports requisites)
Works of art, collectors' pieces or antiques

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Definition of 'vegetable or mineral carving material' for heading 96.02
classification_rules.description: Heading 96.02 covers worked vegetable or mineral carving material. 'Vegetable carving material' = hard seeds, pips, hulls and nuts used for carving (e.g., corozo/tagua nut, dom palm). 'Mineral carving material' = amber, meerschaum, agglomerated amber/meerschaum, jet (black lignite) and mineral substitutes for jet. These must be WORKED (carved, moulded, shaped). Unworked natural materials → their natural material heading.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — 'Prepared knots and tufts for broom/brush making' for heading 96.03
classification_rules.description: Heading 96.03 includes prepared knots and tufts for broom/brush making. These are UNMOUNTED knots/tufts of animal hair, vegetable fibre or other material that are: (a) ready for incorporation without division into brooms/brushes; OR (b) require only minor further processing (trimming to shape at top) to be ready. Loose, unsorted bristle materials not yet in knot/tuft form → classified by material.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Precious metal/stones in Ch.96 articles: two-tier rule
classification_rules.description: Two different rules apply within Ch.96: (1) Articles of headings 96.07–96.14, 96.16–96.20 (headings OTHER than 96.01–96.06 and 96.15): REMAIN in Ch.96 even if wholly or partly of precious metal, cultured pearls or precious stones — the presence of precious materials does NOT move them to Ch.71. (2) Articles of headings 96.01–96.06 and 96.15: classified there only if precious metals/stones/pearls constitute MINOR CONSTITUENTS (i.e., mere decoration/trim). If precious materials are the dominant/essential element → Ch.71.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 96.01/96.02 (carved material articles) vs Ch.71 (precious material articles)
classification_rules.description: Worked ivory, bone, horn, amber, meerschaum, etc. → 96.01/96.02 as long as the carving material gives the article its essential character. If natural/cultured pearls, precious/semi-precious stones or precious metal are merely minor constituents (small inlay, trim) → still 96.01/96.02. If the precious elements are dominant or give essential character → Ch.71. A carved ivory figurine with gold trim → 96.01. A gold brooch with small ivory inlay → Ch.71.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 96.03 (brushes/brooms) vs Ch.90 (specialised dental/medical brushes)
classification_rules.description: Heading 96.03 covers brushes of all kinds including those 'constituting parts of machines, appliances or vehicles'. HOWEVER, brushes specially designed for dentistry or medical/surgical/veterinary purposes (dental picks with brush, surgical scrub brushes) → 90.18. General-purpose toothbrushes → 96.03. The decisive test: is the brush designed for a specific medical/dental/veterinary function? → 90.18. General cleaning/painting/sweeping/grooming brush → 96.03.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 96.08 (pens — ink/felt/fountain/ballpoint) vs 96.09 (pencils, crayons, chalks) vs Ch.33 (cosmetic pencils)
classification_rules.description: Three headings for writing/marking instruments: 96.08 = ink-based pens (ballpoint, felt-tip, fountain, stylograph, propelling pencils with mechanical lead advancement). 96.09 = graphite pencils, crayons, pastels, charcoals, chalks (wax or chalk-based, non-ink). Ch.33 = cosmetic pencils for toilet/beauty use (eyeliner, eyebrow, lip liner). Distinction from 96.09: cosmetic pencils are designed and marketed for body application, not for writing/drawing on paper.

classification_rules.rule: Note 1(e) exception: carved handles of Ch.82 articles presented separately → 96.01/96.02
classification_rules.description: When a knife, fork or other Ch.82 article has a carved/moulded handle of animal or vegetable carving material and is presented as a COMPLETE article → classified in Ch.82 (the handle gives no override). But if the carved handle (or other carved part) is presented SEPARATELY (not assembled with the blade/tool) → then that separate handle → heading 96.01 or 96.02. Assembly state at presentation determines the classification.
